name: Red Sea
climate: Arid desert on both coasts; extremely hot summers, mild winters; minimal rainfall

neighbors.name: Suez Canal
neighbors.relevance: Northern terminus; Red Sea traffic feeds directly into Suez for Europe-bound trade

neighbors.name: Horn of Africa
neighbors.relevance: Southern coast; Djibouti and Eritrea control Bab el-Mandeb approaches

neighbors.name: Levant
neighbors.relevance: Northeastern neighbor; Israel and Jordan have Red Sea port access at Eilat/Aqaba

neighbors.name: Gulf Cooperation Council
neighbors.relevance: Saudi Arabia's western coast dominates the eastern Red Sea shore

neighbors.name: Sahel Belt
neighbors.relevance: Instability in Sudan and Chad spills into Red Sea coastal areas

neighbors.name: Mediterranean
neighbors.relevance: Connected via Suez; Red Sea disruption reroutes Mediterranean-bound shipping

links.description: Red Sea is the mandatory southern approach to the Suez Canal for Asia-Europe trade
Bab el-Mandeb chokepoint controlled by Horn of Africa states; piracy and Houthi threats originate here
Gulf oil transits Hormuz then enters the Red Sea en route to Suez and Europe
When Red Sea is unsafe, shipping reroutes around the Cape adding 10-14 days
Saudi pipelines connect Gulf oil fields to Red Sea export terminals at Yanbu
Saudi NEOM and Red Sea tourism megaprojects drive regional economic development
